_meta.note: ANONYMIZED, English, k>=3-org distilled patterns from Binclusive's dynamic-audit corpus. Failure-shapes clustered offline by LLM (frozen in data/clusters/), gated k>=3-distinct-org and anonymized by deterministic code. Contains NO customer identifiers (org/project ids, elements, urls stripped; frequency as tier only). Regenerate by re-running the distiller.

patterns.failureShape: A button (often icon-only or with only visual content) exposes no accessible name, so assistive tech announces it as an unlabeled "button".
patterns.fix: Give every button discernible text: visible text content, or an aria-label / aria-labelledby for icon-only buttons. Prefer a native <button> over a clickable <div>/<span>.

patterns.failureShape: Decorative icons, icon-font glyphs, or raw unicode characters are announced literally (as character codes or symbol names), polluting a control's accessible name or the reading order.
patterns.fix: Mark decorative icons and separator glyphs aria-hidden="true". Replace raw unicode used as icons with a properly labeled icon element, and give any meaningful icon a text alternative.

patterns.failureShape: A form control (custom select, combobox, switch, radio, checkbox, slider, date picker, quantity stepper) has no programmatic name, or relies on its current value/placeholder, so assistive tech announces it without a label.
patterns.fix: Give every form control a programmatic name: a <label> associated by id (not placeholder-only), or aria-label / aria-labelledby. For custom controls, also expose the correct role and state.

patterns.failureShape: A link exposes no accessible name (icon-only, logo, image-only, or empty), so its purpose and destination are not conveyed.
patterns.fix: Give every link discernible text or an aria-label that names the destination. For image links, the image needs alt text that describes where the link goes. Remove empty placeholder links.

patterns.failureShape: A custom tab, toggle, slider indicator, or current navigation/language item does not expose its selected/pressed/current state programmatically, so assistive tech can't tell which item is active.
patterns.fix: Expose state on custom widgets: aria-selected="true" on the active tab, aria-pressed on toggles, or aria-current="page"/"true" on the current nav, language, or slide. Keep the attribute in sync as the active item changes.

patterns.component: clickable element with no interactive role
patterns.failureShape: An interactive control is built from a non-interactive element (a link, div, span, image, or heading acting as a button, tab, or toggle), so assistive tech announces the wrong role — or none — and keyboard users can't operate it.
patterns.fix: Use the right element for the behavior: a native <button> for actions, real tabs (role="tablist"/"tab"/"tabpanel"), or at minimum the correct role plus keyboard handling and tabindex. Prefer native HTML over re-roled divs/links.

patterns.failureShape: A container is marked aria-hidden="true" but still contains focusable/interactive descendants (often the page chrome behind an open modal), so keyboard and screen-reader users reach controls that are supposed to be hidden.
patterns.fix: Never put aria-hidden="true" on an element that contains focusable content. When a modal is open, mark sibling regions inert (which also removes them from the tab order) rather than aria-hidden alone, or remove the focusable children from the tab order.

patterns.failureShape: An interactive control (search icon, language switcher, listing, menu toggle) is missing from the accessibility tree and tab order entirely, so it cannot be detected or operated by screen-reader or keyboard users — sometimes due to a server block or severe performance issues.
patterns.fix: Ensure interactive controls render as real <button>/<a> elements present in the DOM and accessibility tree, reachable in browse mode and the tab order. Don't gate critical UI behind aria-hidden, role="presentation", or scripts that never expose semantics.

patterns.failureShape: A trigger that shows/hides content (menu, dropdown, accordion, disclosure, "show more") does not expose its expanded/collapsed state or that it has a popup, so users don't know it's openable or whether it's open.
patterns.fix: Put aria-expanded="true|false" on the trigger and toggle it on open/close; add aria-haspopup for menus/dialogs. Associate the trigger with the controlled region via aria-controls. Use a native <button> as the trigger.

patterns.failureShape: A custom widget uses an invalid or contradictory role/ARIA: role="presentation" on an interactive iframe, multiple tabs marked aria-selected at once, a concatenated or wrong accessible name, a dialog with no name, or state attributes leaking between controls.
patterns.fix: Use roles and ARIA attributes only as the spec allows: pick the correct role, keep exactly one selected tab, name dialogs with aria-labelledby, and don't let one control inherit another's value. Prefer native HTML, which carries correct semantics for free.

patterns.failureShape: Multiple navigation landmarks lack unique accessible names (or a landmark is named after its first child), so screen-reader users can't distinguish primary navigation from secondary navigation when listing landmarks.
patterns.fix: Give each repeated navigation landmark a unique, descriptive aria-label or aria-labelledby (e.g. "Main navigation", "Language switcher") that reflects the whole region, not one child item.

patterns.failureShape: A dynamic status, loading state, autocomplete suggestions, validation error, or value change is not exposed to assistive tech (no live region / stale aria-invalid / no value text), so screen-reader users miss the update.
patterns.fix: Announce dynamic changes with a live region (aria-live="polite", or role="status"/"alert"); keep aria-invalid and error messages in sync; use the combobox pattern (aria-expanded/aria-controls/aria-activedescendant) for autocomplete. For value widgets keep aria-valuenow/valuetext current.
